strategy_notes: FULLY COMMITTED to cyclical rotation. Key principles: (1) Pyramiding approach: enter at 15-20% allocation, add 5-10% on confirmation (green day 2-3). Never go full size on initial entry during regime transitions. (2) Keep ONE defensive anchor (ABBV) at ~12% for portfolio stability. (3) Tight stops at 6% on all cyclical positions — if the bear rally thesis fails, exit quickly. (4) Monitor for REVERSAL signals: if SPY drops >2% on any single day with breadth contraction, immediately trim cyclicals back to 50% of position. (5) Sell defensives showing negative relative strength on rally days — confirmed XOM/COST pattern. (6) Geopolitical catalyst: Iran war resolution expectations are driving this rally. If negotiations stall or hostilities escalate, the rally unwinds fast — have exit plan ready. (7) Target 60-70% invested, 30-40% cash as hedge against bear trap.

recent_trades.reason: KO is -0.20% today on a +1.63% SPY day — the weakest relative strength in my portfolio. Only +0.39% gain from entry. KO is a pure defensive play that will underperform if this risk-on move has legs. Selling to raise cash and create portfolio flexibility. If the market reverses back down, I can re-enter KO or similar defensives. If the market continues rallying, I'll have cash to rotate into names showing confirmation of the trend change.

COST was flat (-0.02%) on a +2.91% SPY day — massive relative weakness. Consumer staples/defensive retail is rotating out hard. The +1.13% gain from entry is at risk of evaporating. Locking in small profit and freeing capital for better-positioned names.

XOM was -1.06% on a +2.91% SPY day — worst performer in the portfolio and one of the weakest in the entire universe today. Energy is selling off (CVX -1.81% too), suggesting geopolitical risk premium may be fading as markets rally. This is exactly the de-escalation exit signal my strategy notes flagged. Taking the +0.77% gain before it turns negative.

NVDA +5.59% today — leading the rally with strong semiconductor momentum. AI/datacenter secular tailwind remains intact. If this is a genuine recovery, semis will lead. If it's a bear bounce, the tight stop protects capital. Positioning for cyclical leadership rotation with controlled risk.

META +6.67% today — strongest performer in the top 50. Mega-cap tech is clearly leading this rotation. Strong fundamentals (ad revenue growth, AI investments, cost discipline). If breadth expansion continues over next 3 days, this confirms the trend. Tight stop to protect against bear trap reversal.

XOM is -1.06% on a +2.91% SPY day — the weakest name in the universe. Energy is clearly rotating out as geopolitical risk premium fades (CVX -1.81% too). Already -1.72% from entry. This is exactly the defensive-to-cyclical rotation exit signal. No reason to hold a name showing persistent relative weakness on strong rally days. Free up ~$14.8K for better-positioned names.

COST is flat (-0.02%) on a +2.91% SPY day — massive relative underperformance. Consumer staples are clearly lagging in this risk-on environment. Only +0.97% from entry. Taking the small gain and redeploying into names with stronger momentum. Defensive retail will continue to lag if this rally persists.

Adding to NVDA position using pyramiding approach. NVDA +5.59% today, leading the semiconductor rally. AI/datacenter secular tailwind is intact. If the Iran de-escalation narrative holds, growth/cyclical tech will continue leading. This is a confirmation add — the initial entry was yesterday and it's already confirming with strong momentum. Scaling up per pyramiding research: add to winners showing confirmation.

Adding to META position. +6.67% today — strongest performer in the top 50. Mega-cap tech is leading this rotation with strong ad revenue fundamentals and AI investments. Pyramiding into a winner that's confirming. If this rally has legs, META will be among the biggest beneficiaries of a sentiment shift.

GOOGL +5.14% today — strong participation in the tech rally. Diversifying cyclical exposure across mega-cap tech rather than concentrating in just two names. GOOGL benefits from same AI/advertising tailwinds as META but adds search/cloud diversification. Initial position size per pyramiding strategy — start moderate, add on confirmation next cycle.

last_decision.market_assessment: Strong risk-on rally driven by expectations of Iran war resolution. SPY +2.91%, QQQ +3.39% with mega-cap tech and cyclicals leading (META +6.67%, INTC +7.14%, CAT +6.15%, NVDA +5.59%). Defensives (XOM -1.06%, PEP -0.98%, KO -0.29%, PG -0.19%) are sharply underperforming, confirming a clear rotation from defensive to cyclical leadership. All MACD signals still bearish, which means this could still be a bear market rally, but the breadth and magnitude suggest it has legs — the S&P is down 7% for the quarter and sentiment is shifting on geopolitical de-escalation hopes.
last_decision.self_reflection: My last cycle correctly identified the rotation signal: I sold PEP, KO, COST, and XOM on their relative weakness during the rally day, and bought NVDA and META as cyclical leaders. That was good execution on the barbell transition. However, I notice I re-bought COST and still hold XOM despite having sold them — this appears to be a data inconsistency but I need to act on current positions. XOM is now -1.72% from re-entry and underperforming badly on rally days. The key lesson: when I identify a regime change, I need to commit fully rather than half-measure. My win rate is 60% which is decent, but I need to cut losers faster and let winners run. The pyramiding research confirms I should scale into positions rather than go full-size immediately during uncertain transitions.

last_decision.next_research: Mean reversion vs momentum continuation in post-correction rallies — specifically after 7%+ quarterly drawdowns in the S&P 500, how often do the first-week leaders (mega-cap tech) continue leading in weeks 2-4 versus reverting, and whether profit-taking timing matters more than position selection
